decisions.text: Scope is at the program level; not at tenant-level.

For Clinical Course, preceptor and site evaluations stay within learning activities — CE for clinical courses focuses only on course content/structure, the course coordinator (DCE), and Course Instructor.
Survey responses are anonymous: no student identifier is attached to the submitted response.
Minimum response threshold is 5 — results for any course suppressed until minimum 5 responses received.
1–5 Likert scale enforced as standard across all evaluations. Not a configurable option at faculty or program level.
Evaluation data retained for minimum 7 years to support longitudinal accreditation evidence.
The overall approach is to re-design Surveys in such a way that Course Evaluation and Survey can co-exist.
Three new constructs introduced to enable CE: Survey type (mandatory — Course evaluations or General surveys), Course type (optional — Didactic or Clinical), and Subject (assigned to each section).
We will just track submission and not the responses — survey responses are untraceable to individual students; no IP address, device ID, or response timing metadata exposed.
